title: thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. angustifolia
intro: Thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. angustifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Thymelaeaceae, also known as the spurge family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in North Africa and the Mediterranean region, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Thymelaea tarton-raira subsp. angustifolia are said to include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and providing antioxidant protection. The plant is typically prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, or used in traditional medicine in the form of a poultice or ointment.
